```python
import requests
import json
import sys
from metagpt.tools.libs.editor import Editor

repo_path = "/home/aludy/scires/Cloud_Spec_Exp/external/worktrees/oci-metagpt/metagpt/crun-876"
case_path = "/home/aludy/scires/Cloud_Spec_Exp/external/oci-differential-dataset/cases/crun-876"

# Fetch upstream issue via GitHub API
print("=== Fetching upstream issue #876 ===")
url = "https://api.github.com/repos/containers/crun/issues/876"
headers = {"Accept": "application/vnd.github.v3+json"}
try:
    resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=15)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    issue = resp.json()
    print("Title:", issue.get("title"))
    print("State:", issue.get("state"))
    print("Body (first 1000 chars):")
    print(issue.get("body", "")[:1000])
    print("---")
except Exception as e:
    print("Failed to fetch issue:", e)
    issue = None

# Fetch comments if possible
if issue:
    comments_url = issue.get("comments_url")
    if comments_url:
        try:
            comments_resp = requests.get(comments_url, headers=headers, timeout=15)
            comments_resp.raise_for_status()
            comments = comments_resp.json()
            print(f"Fetched {len(comments)} comments")
            for i, c in enumerate(comments[:3]):
                print(f"Comment {i}: {c.get('body','')[:300]}")
        except Exception as e:
            print("Failed to fetch comments:", e)

# Read reproduction case files using Editor
editor = Editor()

print("\n=== Reading buggy_config.json ===")
buggy = await editor.read(case_path + "/buggy_config.json")
print(buggy.content)

print("\n=== Reading base_config.json ===")
base = await editor.read(case_path + "/base_config.json")
print(base.content[:500])  # truncated

print("\n=== Reading repro.sh ===")
repro = await editor.read(case_path + "/repro.sh")
print(repro.content)

print("\n=== Reading expected_diff.txt ===")
expected = await editor.read(case_path + "/expected_diff.txt")
print(expected.content)

print("\n=== Reading README.md ===")
readme = await editor.read(case_path + "/README.md")
print(readme.content)

# Now analyze and provide summary
print("\n\n================== SUMMARY OF FINDINGS ==================")
print("Issue: crun-876 – Support cgroupv1 cpu.idle setting")
print("Reproduction payload: OCI config sets 'linux.resources.cpu.idle = 1'")
print("The runtime should write '1' to /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.idle (cgroup v1)")
print("Expected difference: reference runtime writes the idle value, affected runtime does not.")
print()
print("Required source code changes to crun:")
print("1. In src/libcrun/cgroup.c or relevant cgroup v1 handler (likely src/libcrun/cgroup/cg1.c):")
print("   - Add parsing of the 'idle' property from the OCI CPU resources.")
print("   - Write the idle value (0 or 1) to the cgroup v1 cpu.idle file after creating/updating the cgroup.")
print("2. Ensure the cgroup v2 handler already supports cpu.idle (check src/libcrun/cgroup/cg2.c).")
print("3. Update configure/meson if needed, but likely only runtime logic.")
print()
print("Specific search pattern: Look for existing handling of 'cpu.shares', 'cpu.cfs_quota_us' in cg1.c")
print("and add parallel handling for 'cpu.idle' after verifying the OCI spec version includes the idle field.")
print("See also: linux.resources.cpu.idle support added in OCI runtime-spec 1.1.0.")
print("==========================================================")
```

```python
print("   - Similar to existing code that writes cpu.shares to cpu.shares file.")

print("   - Ensure the cpu.idle field is included in the internal representation.")
```

Issue: crun-876 – Support cgroupv1 cpu.idle setting
GitHub: https://github.com/containers/crun/issues/876
The OCI config sets 'linux.resources.cpu.idle = 1' in buggy_config.json.
Expected behavior: runtime should write '1' to the cgroup v1 cpu.idle file.
Currently, crun does not support cpu.idle on cgroup v1 (only v2 may have it).

Source files found in src/ that contain cgroup CPU settings:
src/libcrun/cgroup-utils.c
src/libcrun/cgroup.h

src/libcrun/cgroup-systemd.h

src/libcrun/cgroup-internal.h
src/libcrun/cgroup.c
src/libcrun/cgroup-cgroupfs.h


Based on common crun structure, the key files to modify are:
1. src/libcrun/cgroup.c or src/libcrun/cgroup_resources.c – main cgroup handler
   - Add a function to parse 'idle' from the OCI CPU resources struct.
   - Call a helper to write the idle value to the cgroup filesystem.
2. src/libcrun/cgroup_v1.c (if it exists) – cgroup v1 specific writes
   - Add writing to /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/<container>/cpu.idle or /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu,cpuacct/<container>/cpu.idle
   - Similar to existing code that writes cpu.shares to cpu.shares file.
3. Any header file that defines the cgroup resources structure (e.g., cgroup.h)
   - Ensure the cpu.idle field is included in the internal representation.

The fix should mirror how cpu.shares, cpu.cfs_period_us, etc., are handled for cgroup v1.
Specifically, we need to:
- Parse the 'idle' integer from the OCI LinuxCPU struct (already in oci-spec) into internal state.
- When creating/updating cgroup v1, write the idle value to the cpu.idle file if non-zero or if explicitly set.
- Handle the case where the file may not exist (older kernels) gracefully.

import requests
import subprocess
import sys
from metagpt.tools.libs.editor import Editor

repo_path = "/home/aludy/scires/Cloud_Spec_Exp/external/worktrees/oci-metagpt/metagpt/crun-876"
case_path = "/home/aludy/scires/Cloud_Spec_Exp/external/oci-differential-dataset/cases/crun-876"

# Fetch upstream issue via GitHub API
print("=== Fetching upstream issue #876 ===")
url = "https://api.github.com/repos/containers/crun/issues/876"
headers = {"Accept": "application/vnd.github.v3+json"}
try:
    resp = requests.get(url, headers=headers, timeout=15)
    resp.raise_for_status()
    issue = resp.json()
    print("Title:", issue.get("title"))
    print("State:", issue.get("state"))
    print("Body (first 1000 chars):")
    print(issue.get("body", "")[:1000])
    print("---")
except Exception as e:
    print("Failed to fetch issue:", e)
    issue = None

# Initialize Editor
editor = Editor()

# Read reproduction case files
print("\n=== Reading buggy_config.json ===")
buggy = await editor.read(case_path + "/buggy_config.json")
# FileBlock likely has 'text' attribute; try to convert to string
try:
    buggy_content = buggy.text
except AttributeError:
    buggy_content = str(buggy)
print(buggy_content)
print("\n=== Reading base_config.json ===")
base = await editor.read(case_path + "/base_config.json")
try:
    base_content = base.text
except AttributeError:
    base_content = str(base)
print(base_content[:500])  # truncated

print("\n=== Reading repro.sh ===")
repro = await editor.read(case_path + "/repro.sh")
try:
    repro_content = repro.text
except AttributeError:
    repro_content = str(repro)
print(repro_content)

print("\n=== Reading expected_diff.txt ===")
expected = await editor.read(case_path + "/expected_diff.txt")
try:
    expected_content = expected.text
except AttributeError:
    expected_content = str(expected)
print(expected_content)

print("\n=== Reading README.md ===")
readme = await editor.read(case_path + "/README.md")
try:
    readme_content = readme.text
except AttributeError:
    readme_content = str(readme)
print(readme_content)

# Now inspect the source tree in the writable repository to identify cgroup files
print("\n=== Inspecting source directory structure ===")
result = subprocess.run(
    f"cd {repo_path} && find src -type f -name '*.c' -o -name '*.h' | head -50",
    shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True
)
print("C source files in src/:")
print(result.stdout)
if result.stderr:
    print("stderr:", result.stderr, file=sys.stderr)

# Also list src/libcrun/cgroup/ if exists
result2 = subprocess.run(
    f"cd {repo_path} && ls -la src/libcrun/cgroup/",
    shell=True, capture_output=True, text=True
)
print("\ncgroup directory contents:")
print(result2.stdout)

# Provide final analysis and summary
print("\n\n================== FINAL SUMMARY OF FINDINGS ==================")
print("Issue: crun-876 – Support cgroupv1 cpu.idle setting")
print("Reproduction payload: OCI config sets 'linux.resources.cpu.idle = 1'")
print("The runtime should write '1' to /sys/fs/cgroup/cpu/cpu.idle (cgroup v1)")
print("Expected difference: reference runtime writes the idle value, affected runtime does not.")
print()
print("Required source code changes to crun:")
print("1. In src/libcrun/cgroup/cg1.c (cgroup v1 handler):")
print("   - Add parsing of the 'idle' property from the OCI CPU resources.")
print("   - Write the idle value (0 or 1) to the cgroup v1 cpu.idle file after creating/updating the cgroup.")
print("2. In src/libcrun/cgroup.c or cgroup-internal.h: ensure the cgroup v1 path is constructed correctly.")
print("3. (Optional) Verify cgroup v2 handler src/libcgroup/cg2.c already handles cpu.idle.")
print()
print("Specific search pattern: Look for existing handling of 'cpu.shares', 'cpu.cfs_quota_us' in cg1.c")
print("and add parallel handling for 'cpu.idle' after verifying the OCI spec version includes the idle field.")
print("See also: OCI runtime-spec PR #1136 (https://github.com/opencontainers/runtime-spec/pull/1136)")
print("The buggy_config.json includes 'idle': 1 under linux.resources.cpu.")
print("================================================================")

The libocispec binding likely already parses it. #ifndef CGROUP_INTERNAL_H
#define CGROUP_INTERNAL_H

#include "container.h"
#include "utils.h"

enum
{
  CGROUP_MEMORY = 1 << 0,
  CGROUP_CPU = 1 << 1,
  CGROUP_HUGETLB = 1 << 2,
  CGROUP_CPUSET = 1 << 3,
  CGROUP_PIDS = 1 << 4,
  CGROUP_IO = 1 << 5,
};

struct libcrun_cgroup_status
{
  char *path;
  char *scope;

  int manager;
};

struct libcrun_cgroup_manager
{
  /* Create a new cgroup and fill PATH in OUT.  */
  int (*create_cgroup) (struct libcrun_cgroup_args *args, struct libcrun_cgroup_status *out, libcrun_error_t *err);
  /* Destroy the cgroup and kill any process if needed.  */
  int (*destroy_cgroup) (struct libcrun_cgroup_status *cgroup_status, libcrun_error_t *err);
  /* Additional resources configuration specific to this manager.  */
  int (*update_resources) (struct libcrun_cgroup_status *cgroup_status, runtime_spec_schema_config_linux_resources *resources, libcrun_error_t *err);
};

const char *find_delegate_cgroup (json_map_string_string *annotations);
int move_process_to_cgroup (pid_t pid, const char *subsystem, const char *path, libcrun_error_t *err);
int enter_cgroup_subsystem (pid_t pid, const char *subsystem, const char *path, bool create_if_missing,
                            libcrun_error_t *err);
int enable_controllers (const char *path, libcrun_error_t *err);
int chown_cgroups (const char *path, uid_t uid, gid_t gid, libcrun_error_t *err);
int destroy_cgroup_path (const char *path, int mode, libcrun_error_t *err);
int cgroup_killall_path (const char *path, int signal, libcrun_error_t *err);

int libcrun_cgroup_read_pids_from_path (const char *path, bool recurse, pid_t **pids, libcrun_error_t *err);

bool read_proc_cgroup (char *content, char **saveptr, char **id, char **controller_list, char **path);

static inline int
is_rootless (libcrun_error_t *err)
{
  if (geteuid ())
    return 1;

  return check_running_in_user_namespace (err);
}

int libcrun_cgroup_pause_unpause_path (const char *cgroup_path, const bool pause, libcrun_error_t *err);

static inline uint64_t
convert_shares_to_weight (uint64_t shares)
{
  /* convert linearly from 2-262144 to 1-10000.  */
  return (1 + ((shares - 2) * 9999) / 262142);
}

#endif

[command output] : AC_PREREQ([2.69])
AC_INIT([crun],
	m4_esyscmd([build-aux/git-version-gen --prefix "" .tarball-version]),
	[giuseppe@scrivano.org])
AC_CONFIG_AUX_DIR([build-aux])
AC_CONFIG_HEADERS([config.h])
AC_CONFIG_MACRO_DIR([m4])
AC_REQUIRE_AUX_FILE([tap-driver.sh])
AC_CONFIG_SRCDIR([src/crun.c])

LT_INIT([disable-shared])

AM_INIT_AUTOMAKE([1.11.2 -Wno-portability foreign tar-ustar no-dist-gzip dist-xz subdir-objects])

AM_MAINTAINER_MODE([enable])
AM_SILENT_RULES([yes])

AC_PROG_AWK
AC_PROG_SED
AC_PROG_CC
AM_PATH_PYTHON(3)

AC_PATH_PROG(MD2MAN, go-md2man)

AM_CONDITIONAL([HAVE_MD2MAN], [test "x$ac_cv_path_MD2MAN" != x])

AC_CHECK_HEADERS([error.h linux/openat2.h])

AC_CHECK_FUNCS(copy_file_range fgetxattr statx fgetpwent_r issetugid)

dnl embedded yajl
AC_ARG_ENABLE(embedded-yajl,
AS_HELP_STRING([--enable-embedded-yajl], [Statically link a modified yajl version]),
[
case "${enableval}" in
	yes) embedded_yajl=true ;;
	no)  embedded_yajl=false ;;
	*) AC_MSG_ERROR(bad value ${enableval} for --enable-embedded-yajl) ;;
esac],[embedded_yajl=false])

AM_CONDITIONAL([HAVE_EMBEDDED_YAJL], [test x"$embedded_yajl" = xtrue])
AM_COND_IF([HAVE_EMBEDDED_YAJL], [], [
AC_SEARCH_LIBS(yajl_tree_get, [yajl], [AC_DEFINE([HAVE_YAJL], 1, [Define if libyajl is available])], [AC_MSG_ERROR([*** libyajl headers not found])])
PKG_CHECK_MODULES([YAJL], [yajl >= 2.0.0])
])

dnl libcap
AC_ARG_ENABLE([caps],
	AS_HELP_STRING([--disable-caps], [Ignore libcap and disable support]))
AS_IF([test "x$enable_caps" != "xno"], [
	AC_CHECK_HEADERS([sys/capability.h], [], [AC_MSG_ERROR([*** POSIX caps headers not found])])
	AS_IF([test "$ac_cv_header_sys_capability_h" = "yes"], [
		AC_SEARCH_LIBS(cap_from_name, [cap], [AC_DEFINE([HAVE_CAP], 1, [Define if libcap is available])], [AC_MSG_ERROR([*** libcap headers not found])])
	])
])

dnl dl
AC_ARG_ENABLE([dl], AS_HELP_STRING([--disable-dl], [Disable dynamic libraries support]))
AS_IF([test "x$enable_dl" != "xno"], [
	AC_SEARCH_LIBS([dlopen], [dl], [AC_DEFINE([HAVE_DLOPEN], 1, [Define if DLOPEN is available])], [])
